checks.describe: host Ollama serves the synthetic-data generator model qwen3:0.6b (Step 2). Self-seeds by pulling it if absent
checks.run: ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'qwen3:0.6b' || ollama pull qwen3:0.6b >/dev/null 2>&1; if ollama list 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'qwen3:0.6b'; then echo gen-ready; else echo gen-missing; fi

checks.describe: the three pipeline scripts exist — synthesize, train, merge/convert (Steps 2, 3, 5)
checks.run: if [ -s labs/m6/synthesize.py ] && [ -s labs/m6/train_lora.py ] && [ -s labs/m6/merge_and_convert.py ]; then echo scripts-present; else echo scripts-missing; fi

checks.describe: the synthesizer reads only the corpus and never OPENS the held-out golden set — the contamination rule (Step 2). It may mention golden.yaml in comments, but must not read the file
checks.run: F=labs/m6/synthesize.py; if grep -q 'corpus' "$F" && ! grep -qE 'open\([^)]*golden|yaml.safe_load|--golden' "$F"; then echo holdout-clean; else echo holdout-risk; fi

checks.describe: synthesize.py produces chat-format train/eval JSONL from the corpus (Step 2). Self-seeds a small 2-section sample if none present; asserts the chat shape, not a run-specific count
checks.run: T=labs/opsmate/train/train.jsonl; [ -s "$T" ] || OLLAMA_URL=${OLLAMA_URL:-http://localhost:11434} uv run --quiet labs/m6/synthesize.py --max-sections 2 --eval-frac 0.2 >/dev/null 2>&1; if [ -s "$T" ] && head -1 "$T" | jq -e '.messages[0].role == "system" and .messages[2].role == "assistant"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo synth-ok; else echo synth-bad; fi

checks.describe: the trainer uses the spike LoRA config — r=8, alpha=16, q_proj+v_proj — and prints the trainable-parameter line (Step 3)
checks.run: F=labs/m6/train_lora.py; if grep -q 'q_proj' "$F" && grep -q 'v_proj' "$F" && grep -q 'print_trainable_parameters' "$F"; then echo lora-config-ok; else echo lora-config-bad; fi

checks.describe: training writes progress.jsonl (one line per step, the X-Ray Train lens tails it) and the loss descends across the run — last loss below first (Step 3, Step 4). Self-seeds a short real train if no progress log exists; asserts the shape, not a target loss
checks.run: P=labs/opsmate/train/progress.jsonl; if [ ! -s "$P" ]; then T=labs/opsmate/train/train.jsonl; [ -s "$T" ] || OLLAMA_URL=${OLLAMA_URL:-http://localhost:11434} uv run --quiet labs/m6/synthesize.py --max-sections 4 --eval-frac 0.2 >/dev/null 2>&1; uv run --quiet labs/m6/train_lora.py --epochs 6 >/dev/null 2>&1; fi; F=$(head -1 "$P" 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.loss // empty' 2>/dev/null); L=$(tail -1 "$P" 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.loss // empty' 2>/dev/null); N=$(grep -c '"step"' "$P" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$F" ] && [ -n "$L" ] && [ "$N" -ge 2 ] 2>/dev/null && awk "BEGIN{exit !($L < $F)}"; then echo loss-descends; else echo loss-flat; fi

checks.describe: the LoRA adapter is saved and is a small artifact (a few MB) — the removable-fittings / M7-packages-this point (Step 3)
checks.run: D=labs/opsmate/train/adapter; if [ -f "$D/adapter_model.safetensors" ] && [ -f "$D/adapter_config.json" ]; then echo adapter-saved; else echo adapter-missing; fi

checks.describe: the compose model service serves a switchable GGUF file via MODEL_GGUF, defaulting to the base model (Step 6)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/compose.yaml; if grep -q 'MODEL_GGUF:-qwen3-0.6b-q8_0.gguf' "$F"; then echo gguf-switchable; else echo gguf-fixed; fi

checks.describe: the merge+convert step produced a servable tuned GGUF next to the base one (Step 5). Self-seeds merge+convert from the adapter if absent; asserts the file exists and starts with the GGUF magic
checks.run: G=labs/opsmate/models/gguf/opsmate-tuned-q8_0.gguf; if [ ! -f "$G" ] && [ -d labs/opsmate/train/adapter ]; then uv run --quiet labs/m6/merge_and_convert.py >/dev/null 2>&1; fi; if [ -f "$G" ] && [ "$(head -c 4 "$G" 2>/dev/null)" = "GGUF" ]; then echo tuned-gguf-ok; else echo tuned-gguf-missing; fi

checks.describe: the baseline template carries the M6 rows so the learner can record tuned-vs-baseline numbers into baseline-mine.md (Step 7)
checks.run: F=labs/opsmate/evals/baseline-template.md; if grep -qi 'M6' "$F" && grep -qi 'tuned' "$F"; then echo m6-rows-present; else echo m6-rows-missing; fi
